revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} **Kuno Kokuzō** (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} **Kuno Kokuzō** (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} **Kuno Kokuzō** (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} **Kuno Kokuzō** (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} **Kuno Kokuzō** (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} **Kuno Kokuzō** (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} **Kuno Kokuzō** (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kuno no Kuni {{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} **Kuno Kokuzō** (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kuno no Kuni {{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} **Kuno Kokuzō** (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kuno no Kuni {{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} **Kuno Kokuzō** (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kuno no Kuni {{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} **Kuno Kokuzō** (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kuno no Kuni {{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kuno no Kuni {{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} **Kuno Kokuzō** (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kuno no Kuni {{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kuno no Kuni {{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kuno no Kuni {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kuno no Kuni {{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko-qq]] revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kuno revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kuno revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuno no Kuni no
