{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga {{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuno no Kuni no revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuno no Kuni no {{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuno no Kuni no {{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga {{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuno no Kuni no revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuno no Kuni no {{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kokuzō]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:久努国造]] [[en:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏|12=draft}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫|12=draft}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|12=draft}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造|12=draft}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|12=draft}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|12=draft}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|12=draft}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造|12=draft}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|12=draft}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|12=draft}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|12=draft}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|12=draft}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|12=draft}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|12=draft}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市|12=draft}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|12=draft}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|12=draft}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|12=draft}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|12=draft}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|12=draft}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kokuzō]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:久努国造]] [[en:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏|12=draft}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫|12=draft}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|12=draft}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造|12=draft}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|12=draft}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|12=draft}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|12=draft}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造|12=draft}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|12=draft}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|12=draft}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|12=draft}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|12=draft}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|12=draft}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|12=draft}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市|12=draft}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|12=draft}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|12=draft}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|12=draft}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|12=draft}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|12=draft}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Shizuoka}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kokuzō]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:久努国造]] [[en:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏|12=draft}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫|12=draft}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|12=draft}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造|12=draft}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|12=draft}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏|12=draft}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|12=draft}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|12=draft}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造|12=draft}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|12=draft}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄命|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|12=draft}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|12=draft}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|12=draft}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|12=draft}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|12=draft}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市|12=draft}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|12=draft}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|12=draft}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|12=draft}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|12=draft}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|12=draft}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|12=draft}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:久努国造]] [[en:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|伊香色雄" to "|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o"
revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:久努国造]] [[en:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

revision.comment: 13 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:久努国造 up to rev 103130707
revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 |家名 = 久努国造 |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[久能氏|久努氏]]（直） |家祖 = [[印幡足尼]] |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天孫]]） |出身地 = [[大和国|大倭国]] |根拠地 = 久努国（のちの[[遠江国]]） |人物 = [[#人物]]参照 |支流 = （括弧内は姓）
[[遠淡海国造]]
[[佐夜氏]]（直）
[[珠流河国造]]
[[金刺氏]]
[[小市国造]]
[[越智氏|小市氏]]（直）
[[風速国造]]
[[風速氏]]（直） }} '''久努国造'''（くののくにみやつこ/くぬのくにのみやつこ・くのこくぞう/くぬこくぞう）は[[#支配領域|久努国]]（遠江国中部）を支配した[[国造]]。 == 概要 == === 表記 === 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」に'''久努国造'''とある。 === 祖先 === *『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」には[[物部氏|物部連]]の祖[[伊香色雄命]]の孫'''[[印幡足尼]]'''（いにばすくね、いなばすくね）が[[仲哀天皇]]朝に任じられたとある。 *「天孫本紀」、「国造本紀」には伊香色雄命または[[物部十千根|十千根]][[大連]]の子とされる'''印岐美連公'''は「志紀県主、遠江国造、久努直、佐夜直等の祖」とあり、同書記載の新河大連の子で、佐夜部直、久努直等の祖とされる'''物部大小木宿禰'''とも同人と考えられる[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm 沼津の高尾山古墳の保存問題]」『古樹紀之房間』、2015年。。また印播足尼は世代的・地域的・系図的に印岐美命の子にあたると考えられる宝賀寿男「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm 「天孫本紀」物部氏系譜の検討]」『古樹紀之房間』、2008年。 === 氏族 === '''[[久能氏|久努氏]]'''（くぬうじ、くのうじ、[[姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）で、[[佐夜氏]]と同族とされる。 == 本拠 == 国造の本拠はのちの[[遠江国]][[山名郡]]の'''[[久努村|久努郷]]'''で、現在の[[静岡県]][[袋井市]]鷲巣・久野とされる[[下中真人]]「山名郡」『静岡県の地名　日本歴史地名大系22』[[平凡社]]、2000年。。近辺には国本や久野といった地名があり、久野城跡なども残る。 === 支配領域 === 久努国造の支配領域は当時'''久努国'''と呼ばれていた地域であり、後の山名郡（袋井市）、[[周智郡]]、[[佐野郡]]一帯を支配していたとされる。隣の佐野郡[[曽我村 (静岡県)|曽我]]を[[素賀国造]]の本拠とする説もあるが下中真人「佐野郡」『静岡県の地名　日本歴史地名大系22』平凡社、2000年。、もともと山名郡は[[養老]]六年に佐益郡（佐野郡、佐夜郡）から8郷を割いて設置したとされ[[菅野真道]]等「養老六年二月十六日条」『[[続日本紀]]』、797年。、本来久努国造の領域は山名郡と佐野郡であったことになる。 == 氏神 == '''[[赤尾渋垂郡辺神社]]'''（あかおしぶたれこうりべじんじゃ）か宝賀寿男「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm 久野氏・原氏の一族]」『古樹紀之房間』、2002年。。境内に[[印岐美命]]を祀る。 === 関連神社 === *'''[[七ツ森神社]]'''（ななつもりじんじゃ） *:袋井市国本に鎮座する神社で、久努国造を祀る。 === 墓 === *'''[[蔵王権現神社古墳]]'''（ざおうごんげんじんじゃこふん） *:全長49m、高さ4mの[[前方後方墳]]。[[古墳時代]]前期（[[4世紀]]）の築造で、印幡足尼の墓と考えられる宝賀寿男「沼津の高尾山古墳の保存問題」『古樹紀之房間』、2015年。。 *'''[[和田岡古墳群]]'''（わだおかこふんぐん） **:'''各和金塚古墳'''（かくわかなつかこふん） - 全長66mの[[前方後円墳]]で、[[5世紀]]初頭の築造とされる。 **:'''瓢塚古墳'''（ひさごつかこふん） - 全長63mの前方後円墳で、5世紀前半の築造とされる。 **:'''行人塚古墳'''（ぎょうにんつかこふん） - 全長43mの前方後円墳で、築造時期は4世紀とも5世紀ともされる。 **:'''吉岡大塚古墳'''（よしおかおおつかこふん） - 全長55mの前方後円墳で、5世紀中期の築造とされる。 == 参考文献 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]] *[[素賀国造]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=静岡県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くののくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:国造]] [[Category:遠江国|くにのみやつこ]] [[Category:出雲氏|*くののくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:物部氏|-くののくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:静岡県の歴史]]

全長66mの[[前方後円墳]]で、[[5世紀]]初頭の築造とされる。 **:'''瓢塚古墳'''（ひさごつかこふん） - 全長63mの前方後円墳で、5世紀前半の築造とされる。 **:'''行人塚古墳'''（ぎょうにんつかこふん） - 全長43mの前方後円墳で、築造時期は4世紀とも5世紀ともされる。 **:'''吉岡大塚古墳'''（よしおかおおつかこふん） - 全長55mの前方後円墳で、5世紀中期の築造とされる。 == 参考文献 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]] *[[素賀国造]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=静岡県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くののくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:国造]] [[Category:遠江国|くにのみやつこ]] [[Category:出雲氏|*くののくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:物部氏|-くののくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:静岡県の歴史]]

{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o命}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kokuzō]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:久努国造]] [[en:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} {{translated page|ja|久努国造|version=103130707|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|qq=draft}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造|qq=draft}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o命|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|qq=draft}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|qq=draft}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|qq=draft}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市|qq=draft}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|qq=draft}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|qq=draft}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|qq=draft}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|qq=draft}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{translated page|ja|久努国造|version=103130707|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kokuzō]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:久努国造]] [[en:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]

revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno clan|qq=}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson|qq=}}) |Origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} |Base = Kuno Province (later {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}}) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Kokuzō|qq=}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasa clan|qq=}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Kokuzō|qq=}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan|qq=}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya Kokuzō|qq=}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|qq=}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō|qq=}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Ikachine|qq=}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana District, Tōtōmi|qq=}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno village|qq=}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuroi, Shizuoka|qq=}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heibonsha|qq=}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuchi District, Shizuoka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadaoka Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisagotuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōnintuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{translated page|ja|久努国造|version=103130707|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kokuzō]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:久努国造]] [[en:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]

District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadaoka Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 66-meter-long {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisagotuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōnintuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Kokuzō|qq=}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=}}
