{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|qq=}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the {{ill|Kokuzō|ja|国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō|qq=}} of Kuno Province, located in the central region of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}}. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=}} of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}}, was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Ikachine|qq=}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana District, Tōtōmi|qq=}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno village|qq=}}, near modern {{ill|Fukuroi, Shizuoka|ja|袋井市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuroi, Shizuoka|qq=}} in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heibonsha|qq=}}, 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), {{ill|Shuchi District, Shizuoka|ja|周智郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuchi District, Shizuoka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, {{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan|qq=}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya Kokuzō|qq=}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|qq=}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Kuno Province, located in the central region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=}} of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Ikachine|qq=}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana District, Tōtōmi|qq=}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno village|qq=}}, near modern [[:en:Fukuroi, Shizuoka|Fukuroi, Shizuoka]] in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), [[:en:Shūchi District, Shizuoka|Shuchi District, Shizuoka]], and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadaoka Kofun Group|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 66-meter-long [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisagotuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōnintuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{translated page|ja|久努国造|version=103130707|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kokuzō]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:久努国造]] [[en:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]

{{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久野氏}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson|qq=}}) |Origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] |Base = Kuno Province (later [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Kokuzō|qq=}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Kokuzō|qq=}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan|qq=}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya Kokuzō|qq=}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Kuno Province, located in the central region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=}} of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Ikachine|qq=}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana District, Tōtōmi|qq=}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, near modern [[:en:Fukuroi, Shizuoka|Fukuroi, Shizuoka]] in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), [[:en:Shūchi District, Shizuoka|Shuchi District, Shizuoka]], and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadaoka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A 66-meter-long [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisagotuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōnintuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{translated page|ja|久努国造|version=103130707|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kokuzō]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:久努国造]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q85875681]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clan |Name = Kuno Kokuzō |Crest = |Crest Name = |Main Surname = {{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久能氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久野氏|1=Kuno clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369758}} (Nao) |Founder = Inaba Sukune |Type = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Tenson|ja|天孫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson|qq=|1=Posterity of Heaven|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108278353}}) |Origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] |Base = Kuno Province (later [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]) |Notable Figures = See {{section link|#Notable Figures}} |Branches = (Parentheses denote clan suffix)
{{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Kokuzō|qq=|1=Tōtomi Province no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642135}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏|1=Kanasa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646498}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Kokuzō|qq=|1=Koichi Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan|qq=}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya Kokuzō|qq=|1=Kazahaya Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652739}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Kuno Province, located in the central region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}} of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Ikachine|qq=|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24885356}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Yamana district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467644}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, near modern [[:en:Fukuroi, Shizuoka|Fukuroi, Shizuoka]] in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), [[:en:Shūchi District, Shizuoka|Shuchi District, Shizuoka]], and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Saya district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385111}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadaoka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A 66-meter-long [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisagotuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōnintuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} {{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏|1=Kanasa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646498}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Kokuzō|qq=|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan|qq=}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya Kokuzō|qq=|1=kazahayanokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652739}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Kuno Province, located in the central region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}} of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Ikachine|qq=|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24885356}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Yamana district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467644}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, near modern [[:en:Fukuroi, Shizuoka|Fukuroi, Shizuoka]] in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), [[:en:Shūchi District, Shizuoka|Shuchi District, Shizuoka]], and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Saya district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385111}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506353}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadaoka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A 66-meter-long [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisagotuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōnintuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:久努国造]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[:en:Fukuroi, Shizuoka|Fukuroi, Shizuoka]] in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), [[:en:Shūchi District, Shizuoka|Shuchi District, Shizuoka]], and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Saya district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385111}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506353}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506354}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadaoka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisagotuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th {{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Kuno Province, located in the central region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}} of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Ikachine|qq=|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24885356}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Yamana district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467644}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, near modern [[:en:Fukuroi, Shizuoka|Fukuroi, Shizuoka]] in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), [[:en:Shūchi District, Shizuoka|Shuchi District, Shizuoka]], and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Saya district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385111}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506353}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506354}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to {{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Kokuzō|qq=|1=Tōtomi Province no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642135}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏|1=Kanasa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646498}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Kokuzō|qq=|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan|qq=}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya Kokuzō|qq=|1=kazahayanokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652739}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Kuno Province, located in the central region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}} of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Ikachine|qq=|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24885356}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Yamana district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467644}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, near modern [[:en:Fukuroi, Shizuoka|Fukuroi, Shizuoka]] in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), [[:en:Shūchi District, Shizuoka|Shuchi District, Shizuoka]], and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Saya district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385111}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506353}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506354}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadaoka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506356}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506358}}: A 66-meter-long [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisagotuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōnintuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506359}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506361}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{translated page|ja|久努国造|version=103130707|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:久努国造]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏|1=Kanasa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646498}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Kokuzō|qq=|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan|qq=}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya Kokuzō|qq=|1=kazahayanokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652739}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Kuno Province, located in the central region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}} of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Ikachine|qq=|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24885356}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Yamana district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467644}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, near modern [[:en:Fukuroi, Shizuoka|Fukuroi, Shizuoka]] in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), [[:en:Shūchi District, Shizuoka|Shuchi District, Shizuoka]], and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Saya district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385111}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506353}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506354}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadaoka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506356}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506358}}: A 66-meter-long [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisagotuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōnintuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506359}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506361}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{translated page|ja|久努国造|version=103130707|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:久努国造]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[sn:Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]

wikidata|WD=Q11467644}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, near modern [[:en:Fukuroi, Shizuoka|Fukuroi, Shizuoka]] in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), [[:en:Shūchi District, Shizuoka|Shuchi District, Shizuoka]], and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Saya district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385111}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506353}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506354}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadaoka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506356}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506358}}: A 66-meter-long [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisagotuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōnintuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506359}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506361}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{translated page|ja|久努国造|version=103130707|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft {{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya Kokuzō|qq=|1=kazahayanokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652739}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Kuno Province, located in the central region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}} of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Ikachine|qq=|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24885356}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Yamana district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467644}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, near modern [[:en:Fukuroi, Shizuoka|Fukuroi, Shizuoka]] in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), [[:en:Shūchi District, Shizuoka|Shuchi District, Shizuoka]], and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Saya district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385111}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506353}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506354}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadaoka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506356}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506358}}: A 66-meter-long [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisagotuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōnintuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506359}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506361}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{translated page|ja|久努国造|version=103130707|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[ja:久努国造]] [[Category:Kuni no {{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Kokuzō|qq=|1=Tōtomi Province no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642135}}
{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao)
{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏|1=Kanasa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646498}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Kokuzō|qq=|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan|qq=}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya Kokuzō|qq=|1=kazahayanokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652739}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Kuno Province, located in the central region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}} of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Ikachine|qq=|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24885356}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Yamana district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467644}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, near modern [[:en:Fukuroi, Shizuoka|Fukuroi, Shizuoka]] in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), [[:en:Shūchi District, Shizuoka|Shuchi District, Shizuoka]], and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Saya district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385111}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506353}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506354}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadaoka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506356}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506358}}: A 66-meter-long [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisagotuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōnintuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506359}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506361}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{translated page|ja|久努国造|version=103130707|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[ja:久努国造]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] {{wikidata link|Q85875681}}

{{ill|Suruga Kokuzō|ja|珠流河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Suruga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573348}}
{{ill|Kanasa clan|ja|金刺氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺部氏|1=Kanasa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646498}}
{{ill|Koichi Kokuzō|ja|小市国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Kokuzō|qq=|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652684}}
{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan|qq=}} (Nao)
{{ill|Kazahaya Kokuzō|ja|風速国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya Kokuzō|qq=|1=kazahayanokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652739}}
{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazahaya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nao) }} '''Kuno Kokuzō''' (久努国造) refers to the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kokuzō]] of Kuno Province, located in the central region of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]]. == Overview == === Name === The name is recorded as **Kuno Kokuzō** in the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'', "Kokuzō Hongi" section. === Ancestors === * According to the ''Kokuzō Hongi,'' Inaba Sukune (印幡足尼), a grandson of {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikaga Shikoo|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o命|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536799}} of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was appointed Kokuzō during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]. * The ''Tenson Hongi'' and ''Kokuzō Hongi'' also mention Inagimi Ren Kimi (印岐美連公), a son of Ikashikone-no-mikoto or {{ill|Mononobe no Ikachine|ja|物部十千根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Ikachine|qq=|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24885356}}, as an ancestor of the {{ill|Shiki Agatanushi|ja|志紀県主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tōtōmi Kokuzō, Kuno Nao, and Saya Nao.Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun in Numazu]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2015. === Clan === The clan was called the **Kuno clan** (久能氏) with the surname Nao (直) and was related to the {{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Headquarters == The Kokuzō's base was in the Kuno Province, later known as {{ill|Yamana District, Tōtōmi|ja|山名郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana District, Tōtōmi|qq=|1=Yamana district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467644}}, specifically in the areas now called {{ill|Kuno village|ja|久努郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, near modern [[:en:Fukuroi, Shizuoka|Fukuroi, Shizuoka]] in Washizu and Kuno.Masato Shimonaka, "Yamana District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 2000. === Territory === The Kuno Kokuzō ruled over the Kuno Province, covering Yamana District (modern Fukuroi), [[:en:Shūchi District, Shizuoka|Shuchi District, Shizuoka]], and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|qq=|1=Saya district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385111}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506353}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506354}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadaoka Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506356}} ** {{ill|Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|ja|各和金塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakuwakanatsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506358}}: A 66-meter-long [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisagotuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōnintuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506359}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506361}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}} * {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|qq=|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shizuoka}} {{translated page|ja|久努国造|version=103130707|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Izumo clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] }} [[ja:久努国造]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] {{wikidata link|Q85875681}}

District (modern Fukuroi), [[:en:Shūchi District, Shizuoka|Shuchi District, Shizuoka]], and {{ill|Sano District, Shizuoka|ja|佐野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano District, Shizuoka|1=Saya district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385111}}. Some theories suggest neighboring {{ill|Soga Kokuzō|ja|素賀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Kokuzō|1=Soga Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85883175}} were based in Sano District, but historical records indicate Yamana District was formed in 722 AD by separating eight villages from Sano District, confirming the Kuno Kokuzō ruled both districts.Shimonaka, "Sano District," in ''Shizuoka Prefecture Place Names,'' Heibonsha, 2000. == Clan Deity == The clan deity was likely worshiped at {{ill|Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|ja|赤尾渋垂郡辺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akao Shibutare Kōribe Shrine|qq=}},Toshio Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/kunosi.htm The Kuno and Hara Clans]," ''Kojiki no Homma,'' 2002. which enshrines Ikagimi-no-mikoto. === Related Shrines === * {{ill|Nanatsumori Shrine|ja|七ツ森神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanatsumori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506353}} : Located in Kuno, Fukuroi, this shrine enshrines the Kuno Kokuzō. === Tombs === * {{ill|Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja|蔵王権現神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen Shrine Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506354}} : A 49-meter-long, 4-meter-high {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, dated to the 4th century and believed to be the tomb of Inaba Sukune.Hōga, "Preservation Issues of Takao-san Kofun," 2015. * {{ill|Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja|和田岡古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadaoka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506356}} early 5th century. ** {{ill|Hisagotuka Kofun|ja|瓢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisagotuka Kofun|qq=}}: A 63-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the early 5th century. ** {{ill|Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja|行人塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōnintuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506359}}: A 43-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun dated to the 4th or 5th century. ** {{ill|Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|吉岡大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshioka Ōtsuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506361}}: A 55-meter-long zenpō-kōen-fun from the mid-5th century. == References == {{Reflist}} {{ill|Tōtōmi Kokuzō|ja|遠淡海国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Kokuzō|1=Tōtomi Province no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642135}}
